source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Noble Jim Day - Structural Damage/Crane

event.top_event: Unintentional discharge of synthetic oil based mud
actions_taken: Assistant Driller contacted Derrickman upon alarm
Crew secured pits and corrected misaligned valves
event.summary: On December 11, 2014, the Noble Jim Day unintentionally discharged 142 barrels of Synthetic Oil Based Mud (SBM) into the Gulf of Mexico during mud pit cleaning operations. The discharge resulted from valve misalignment during the transition between crew changes. Keys to the dump valves were passed between personnel without proper Lock-out/Tag-out documentation. The Derrickman, lacking knowledge of proper valve alignment and receiving poor communication and labeling, misaligned the valves and created a pathway for hydrocarbons to discharge overboard. The Pit Volume Totalizer alarm detected the loss after approximately 5 minutes, at which point the crew secured the pits and corrected the valve alignment.
recommendations: Improve valve labeling and identification on piping systems
Enhance Lock-out/Tag-out procedures and documentation requirements
Update system drawings to accurately reflect valve installation
Conduct pre-tour safety meetings for all crew transitions
Improve training on proper valve alignment procedures
Enhance communication protocols during critical operations
Ensure Job Safety Analysis (JSA) identifies environmental hazards
key_phrases: environmental hazard not identified in JSA

hazards.name: Mud pit system complexity and valve configuration

threats.name: Valve misalignment during pit cleaning operations
Loss of control during crew change transition
Inadequate knowledge of valve alignment procedures

consequences.name: Discharge of 142 barrels of SBM to Gulf of Mexico

controls.name: Lock-out/Tag-out (LOTO) procedures and documentation
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to track valve key custody and prevent unauthorized valve operation
controls.lod_basis: Primary administrative barrier to prevent loss of control during equipment transitions

supporting_text: the keys for Pit #2's overboard dump valves were issued to the on-tour Derrickman by the Assistant Rig Manager, but no entry was made into the rig's Lock-out/Tag-out log book
During the transition, the keys for Pit #2's dump valves were passed on again to the oncoming Derrickman. Again, no entries were entered into the rig's Lock-out/Tag-out log book

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and safe work procedures before job execution
controls.lod_basis: Primary administrative barrier to identify environmental and operational hazards

supporting_text: Although a JSA was done prior to starting the job, the crew members failed to identify any environmental hazards associated with cleaning the pits
The JSA failed to identify the environmental hazards associated with the job

controls.name: Valve labeling and identification
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to ensure correct valve identification and prevent misalignment
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineering barrier to prevent human error in valve alignment

supporting_text: A lack of knowledge on proper valve alignment, poor communication between the crew members, and poor labeling of the valves, led the Derrickman to misalign the valves
Poor Labeling: Piping and valves were not clearly labeled

controls.name: System drawings and documentation
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to provide accurate reference for valve configuration and alignment
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineering barrier to support correct valve alignment

supporting_text: the operators report showed that the drawings that were available to the Derrickman at the time of the incident did not accurately show the installation of the valves
Drawings of the sump system did not accurately reflect the installation

controls.name: Pre-tour safety meeting
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to brief crew on hazards and procedures during shift transitions
controls.lod_basis: Primary administrative barrier to ensure crew awareness during transitions

supporting_text: No pre-tour safety meeting was held for the new crew members

controls.name: Pit Volume Totalizer (PVT) alarm system
controls.barrier_role: Detection and alarm system to identify unexpected volume loss and alert crew
controls.lod_basis: Secondary detection barrier to identify discharge and trigger corrective action

supporting_text: Upon coming on tour, the AD had set the Pit Volume Totalizer (PVT) to 5 barrels. This alarm would notify the crew if the volume in the pits fluctuated by more than 5 barrels
Once the 5 barrel mark had been reached, the PVT alarm sounded and the AD contacted the Derrickman to notify him of their losses
The crew proceeded to secure the pits and correct the misaligned valves. The total time to secure all valves was approximately five minutes

controls.name: Radio communication protocol during valve operations
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure clear communication between crew members during critical operations
controls.lod_basis: Primary administrative barrier to prevent miscommunication during valve operations

supporting_text: Poor Communication: Radio communication was not utilized while opening the dump valves

controls.name: Training on valve alignment procedures
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure personnel competence in proper valve alignment
controls.lod_basis: Primary administrative barrier to build personnel knowledge and prevent human error

supporting_text: A lack of knowledge on proper valve alignment
Training: Poor understanding of proper alignment for cleaning operation

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
